supply_chain.dependencies: Company relies on independent subcontractors for most aspects of land development and home construction. Subcontractors select and obtain building materials in many cases.
supply_chain.risks: Shortages of materials and skilled labor encountered during 2022. Supply chain disruptions during pandemic. Subcontractors may use improper construction processes or defective materials. Defective products widely used by homebuilding industry can result in extensive repairs. Subcontractor failures to comply with applicable laws can expose company to liability.

regulatory_risks: Local, state and federal statutes, ordinances, rules and regulations relating to zoning, construction permits, construction materials, density, building design, property elevation, building codes and waste handling
Laws requiring use of construction materials that reduce energy consumption (e.g., California Energy Commission requirement for rooftop solar panels)
Requirements to provide roads and other offsite infrastructure prior to commencement of construction
Assessments for schools, parks, streets, highways and other public improvements
Governmental agencies attempting to make homebuilders responsible for violations of wage and other labor laws by subcontractors
Environmental laws including storm water and surface water management, soil, groundwater and wetlands protection, subsurface conditions and air quality protection
Slow growth initiatives and ballot measures that could restrict residential development
FHA and VA regulations for mortgage insurance compliance
State statutory disclosure requirements for marketing and sale of new homes
State licensing requirements for contractors and real estate agents

Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act requirements for mortgage lending and securitizations including minimum lender practice standards, fee limitations, and risk retention requirements
Federal Fair Debt Collection Practices Act and comparable state statutes regulating consumer debt collection
Federal Consumer Financial Protection Bureau regulations regarding residential mortgage loans
Government mandates, standards or regulations intended to reduce greenhouse gas emissions or climate change impacts
Changes in tax laws that could eliminate or reduce income tax benefits of homeownership (mortgage interest deductibility, property tax deductibility)
U.S. anti-corruption laws for international dealings
Privacy and data protection laws and regulations

sanction_risks: International conflicts involving Ukraine and Israel may lead to market disruptions including volatility in commodity prices, credit and capital markets, and supply chain interruptions
Closure or limitation on use of significant shipping routes as a result of conflicts may interrupt supply of key raw materials used in products incorporated in homes
Tariffs and immigration policies recently added to list of concerns confronting homebuilding industry
Possibility that increased tariffs will increase cost of production materials
Possible widespread deportations could affect labor availability
